description: Artefact 5 CQ regression suite. One SPARQL test per competency question, run against music_vocabulary_comprehensive.ttl + tests/test_data.ttl. Pass condition is membership-based (robust to the illustrative real catalog): each designated yes-instance must appear in the result and each no-instance must not. `var` is the projected variable whose values are checked; `contains`/`excludes` are local names (the bit after '#').

tests.question: Which artists share at least one top-level genre with a seed artist?
tests.sample_row: TST_SoloB shares the Rock root with TST_SoloA

tests.question: Which musical agents (artists/bands only, not their songs or albums) share a top-level genre with a seed artist, and which genre name(s) are shared? (Presentation refinement of CQ-1: restricts ?other to :MusicalAgent and emits genre rdfs:labels via GROUP_CONCAT instead of a count.)
tests.sparql: SELECT ?other (GROUP_CONCAT(DISTINCT ?genreName; SEPARATOR=", ") AS ?Shared_Genres) WHERE { :TST_SoloA :hasGenre/:hasBroaderGenre* ?root . ?root a :TopLevelGenre ; rdfs:label ?genreName . ?other a/rdfs:subClassOf* :MusicalAgent ; :hasGenre/:hasBroaderGenre* ?root ; rdfs:label ?label . FILTER(?other != :TST_SoloA) } GROUP BY ?other
tests.sample_row: TST_BandAlt (AlternativeRock -> Rock) shares Shared_Genres="Rock"; the Rock-genre works TST_Album1/TST_Song1 are excluded by the :MusicalAgent restriction, proving agents-only behaviour

tests.question: Which agents are within two hops via collaboratesWith or shared band membership?
Which artists are signed to the same label as a seed artist?
Which works share a seed work's release decade and a top-level genre?
tests.sparql: SELECT DISTINCT ?work WHERE { :TST_Album1 :releasedIn ?y0 ; :hasGenre/:hasBroaderGenre* ?root . ?root a :TopLevelGenre . ?work :releasedIn ?y ; :hasGenre/:hasBroaderGenre* ?root . FILTER(FLOOR(xsd:integer(STR(?y0))/10) = FLOOR(xsd:integer(STR(?y))/10) && ?work != :TST_Album1) }
tests.sample_row: TST_Album2 (1987, Rock) shares the 1980s+Rock with TST_Album1 (1985)

tests.question: Which musicians play the same instrument or instrument family as a seed musician?
tests.sparql: SELECT DISTINCT ?m WHERE { :TST_MusM :hasInstrument ?i . ?i a ?fam . ?m :hasInstrument ?i2 . ?i2 a ?fam . FILTER(?m != :TST_MusM) }
tests.sample_row: TST_SoloA (Guitar, exact) and TST_MusBass (Bass, same PluckedString family)

tests.question: Which artists span more than one top-level genre?
tests.sparql: SELECT ?artist (COUNT(DISTINCT ?root) AS ?n) WHERE { ?artist :hasGenre/:hasBroaderGenre* ?root . ?root a :TopLevelGenre . } GROUP BY ?artist HAVING(COUNT(DISTINCT ?root) > 1)

tests.question: Which candidates are related to a seed by a shared genre, label, or instrument (with evidence)?
tests.sparql: SELECT DISTINCT ?candidate WHERE { { :TST_SoloA :hasGenre/:hasBroaderGenre* ?r . ?r a :TopLevelGenre . ?candidate :hasGenre/:hasBroaderGenre* ?r } UNION { :TST_SoloA :isSignedTo ?l . ?candidate :isSignedTo ?l } UNION { :TST_SoloA :hasInstrument ?i . ?candidate :hasInstrument ?i } FILTER(?candidate != :TST_SoloA) }
tests.sample_row: TST_SoloB shares Rock genre and TST_Label1 with TST_SoloA

tests.question: Which albums share a seed album's producer?
Which entities belong to a top-level genre or any of its subgenres (transitive)?
Which songs reached the #1 chart position?
What other works are associated with a band's members?
Which artists originate from the same place as a seed artist?
tests.sample_row: TST_SoloB and TST_SoloA both originate from TST_CityX

tests.question: Which artists were active during the same time period as a seed artist (overlapping activity interval) AND share a top-level genre? (e.g. 90s Hip Hop peers)
tests.sparql: SELECT DISTINCT ?other WHERE { :TST_Era90sA :activeFrom ?af0 ; :hasGenre/:hasBroaderGenre* ?root . ?root a :TopLevelGenre . OPTIONAL { :TST_Era90sA :activeUntil ?au0 } BIND(xsd:integer(STR(?af0)) AS ?f0) BIND(COALESCE(xsd:integer(STR(?au0)), 9999) AS ?u0) ?other :activeFrom ?af ; :hasGenre/:hasBroaderGenre* ?root . OPTIONAL { ?other :activeUntil ?au } BIND(xsd:integer(STR(?af)) AS ?f) BIND(COALESCE(xsd:integer(STR(?au)), 9999) AS ?u) FILTER(?other != :TST_Era90sA) FILTER(?f0 <= ?u && ?f <= ?u0) }
tests.sample_row: TST_Era90sB (active 1995-2003, Rock) overlaps seed TST_Era90sA (1991-1998, Rock); TST_Era80s misses the era, TST_Era90sJazz misses the genre

tests.question: Which artists share a geographic ancestor at a chosen level (here nation) with a seed artist, via the :locatedIn roll-up? (same-country / same-continent discovery)
tests.sample_row: TST_SoloC (Test City Y -> Testland) shares the nation Testland with seed TST_SoloA; TST_GeoFar is on the same continent but a different nation

tests.question: Which artists came of age (their ~15-25 window overlaps the event) during a historical event, and originated from within the event's place? (derived, not asserted)
tests.sparql: SELECT DISTINCT ?artist WHERE { :TST_EventE gist:actualStartDate ?es ; gist:actualEndDate ?ee ; :locatedIn ?ep . ?artist :bornOn ?b ; :originatesFrom/:locatedIn* ?ep . BIND(YEAR(?b) AS ?by) FILTER(?by + 15 <= YEAR(?ee) && YEAR(?es) <= ?by + 25) }
tests.sample_row: TST_ComeOfAge (born 1948, from Testland) came of age during Test Event E (1960-1968); TST_TooYoung (born 1965) is too young, TST_WrongPlace originates outside Testland

tests.question: Which works belong to a given curated work collection? (browse a box set / anthology / thematic collection)
tests.sample_row: TST_Collection collects TST_Album1 and TST_Song1; TST_Album3/TST_Song2 are not in it
